title: Estimating an earth seismic response
abstract: Estimating an earthresponsecan includedeconvolving a multi-dimensional source wavefieldfrom near-continuously recorded seismic data recorded ata receiver position. The deconvolving can include spreading the near-continuously recorded seismic dataacross a plurality ofpossible source emission angles. The result of the deconvolution can bethe earth response estimate.
claims:
Claims Claims  What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: estimating an earth response by deconvolving a multi-dimensional source wavefield from near-continuously recorded seismic data recorded at a receiver position, the deconvolving comprising: spreading the near-continuously recorded seismic data across a plurality of source emission angles; and wherein a result of the deconvolution is the earth response estimate. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein estimating the earth response further comprises: extracting a set of coherent signals from the earth response estimate; and wherein the set of coherent signals is an additional earth response estimate. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein estimating the earth response further comprises: determining a contribution of the additional earth response estimate to the near- continuously recorded seismic data recorded at the receiver position; subtracting the contribution from the near-continuously recorded seismic data; in response to the subtracting, generating a different deconvolution result by deconvolving the multi -dimensional source wavefield from remaining near-continuously recorded seismic data spread across a different plurality of source emission angles; extracting a different set of coherent signals from the different deconvolution result; and adding the different set of coherent signals to the additional earth response estimate. 4. The method of claim 3, further comprising iteratively performing the determining of the contribution of the additional earth response estimate, the subtracting of the contribution from the near-continuously recorded seismic data, the generating of the different deconvolution result, the extracting of the different set of coherent signals, and the adding of the different set of coherent signals to the earth response estimate. 5. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the plurality of source emission angles comprise possible source emission angles. 6. The method of claim 3, wherein the different plurality of source emission angles comprise source emission angles possible subsequent to the subtracting. 7. A system, comprising: near-continuously recorded seismic data; an extraction engine configured to estimate an earth response by extracting coherent signals from a deconvolution of the near-continuously recorded seismic data iteratively until, based on a threshold, coherent signals are no longer extractable; a first deconvolution engine configured to calculate, in response to the coherent signals being no longer extractable, an angle-dependent first receiver wavefield by deconvolving a source wavefield associated with the near-continuously recorded seismic data with the extracted coherent signals; a residual engine configured to spread a residual receiver trace across a plurality of source emission angles and add the residual receiver trace to a second receiver wavefield associated with a receiver that near-continuously recorded the seismic data; and a second deconvolution engine configured to update the earth response estimate by performing a source emission angle-dependent deconvolution of the source wavefield from the receiver wavefield. 8. The system of claim 7, wherein the plurality of source emission angles comprise all possible source emission angles. 9. The system of claim 7 or claim 8, wherein the near-continuously recorded seismic data is data previously recorded in a receiver position and comprises a near-continuous receiver trace. 10. The system of any of claims 7 to 9, wherein the extraction engine is further configured to determine a different plurality of source emission angles at which the near-continuously recorded seismic data was emitted. 11. The system of any of claims 7 to 10, further comprising a correction engine configured to deconvolve the near-continuously recorded seismic data using a multi-dimensional phase correction and a one-dimensional amplitude correction. 12. The system of any of claims 7 to 1 1 , wherein the deconvolution of the near-continuously recorded seismic data is based on a common receiver gather with the estimated earth response, and wherein the common receiver gather comprises the near-continuously recorded seismic data, the source wavefield, and an input receiver trace associated with the near-continuously recorded seismic data. 13. A method, comprising: receiving seismic data recorded near-continuously at a point receiver as a near- continuous receiver trace; estimating an earth response by extracting source emission data from the near- continuously recorded seismic data, the extracting comprising: deconvolving the seismic data by spreading receiver signals across a plurality of source emission angles; extracting coherent signals from a result of the deconvolution of the seismic data; iteratively performing until no coherent signals are extractable: determining a contribution of the extracted coherent signals to the near- continuous receiver trace; deconvolving a first source wavefield from a residual receiver trace; and extracting coherent signals from a result of the deconvolution of the first source wavefield; and estimating an updated earth response by deconvolving a second source wavefield associated with the near-continuously recorded seismic data based on the iterative performance. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising adding the residual receiver trace to a signal model, wherein adding the residual receiver trace to the signal model comprises: 

 spreading the residual receiver trace across a second plurality of source emission angles; and deconvolving the first source wavefield based on the spreading of the residual receiver trace. 15. The method of claim 13 or claim 14, further comprising correcting for motion of the point receiver as a function of time based on a distance the point receiver moved relative to a start of the near-continuous recording of the seismic data. 16. The method of any of claims 13 to 15, wherein extracting coherent signals comprises prioritizing extraction of coherent signals within the deconvolution result based on an energy of the coherent signals. 17. The method of any of claims 13 to 15, wherein extracting coherent signals comprises prioritizing extraction of coherent signals falling along hyperbolic trajectories within a specified velocity range. 18. The method of any of claims 13 to 15, wherein extracting coherent signals comprises identifying and suppressing incoherent signals. 19. A method to manufacture a geophysical data product, the method comprising: obtaining geophysical data; and processing the geophysical data to generate the geophysical data product, wherein processing the geophysical data comprises: estimating an earth response by deconvolving a multi-dimensional source wavefield from near-continuously recorded seismic data recorded at a receiver position, the deconvolving comprising: spreading the near-continuously recorded seismic data across a plurality of source emission angles; and using a result of the deconvolution as the earth response estimate; and recording the geophysical data product on a non-transitory machine-readable medium. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein processing the geophysical data comprises processing the geophysical data offshore or onshore.
